description: maxIterations as the SOLE loop terminator, over a wildcard (always-more) mock. A pagination loop whose operation always emits another page with a nextCursor, so nothing in the data ever stops the cycle: the each node's maxIterations is the only bound. This makes the exact iteration count observable, so an off-by-one in the cap check (>= relaxed to >) changes the count instead of silently passing. fetch is an each node with maxIterations 3; it feeds a collector buffer and, via a nextCursor filter, cycles back into itself. The buffer flushes at completion and a count transform reports how many pages were fetched: exactly 3. Under a >-instead-of->= bound the loop runs one extra iteration and the count becomes 4, so this fixture goes red against that defect (which the older finite-response corpus fixtures, whose mocks stopped the loop themselves, could not catch).

fixtures.name: maxIterations is the sole loop terminator (wildcard mock)
fixtures.description: fetchPage is a wildcard mock: every invocation emits {page:'P', nextCursor:'c'}, so the nextCursor filter always passes and the cycle never terminates on its own. fetch (each, maxIterations 3) opens invocations for lineage counts 1, 2, 3; the fourth arrival exceeds the cap and is dropped. Each page is accumulated in the collect buffer, which flushes at completion; count reports $count over the batch. The output is exactly [{n:3}]. Because only the cap stops the loop, an off-by-one in the maxIterations check (>= weakened to >) produces {n:4} and fails this fixture.

graph.description: A bounded pagination cycle: an each node feeds a collector buffer and, through a nextCursor filter, cycles back into itself. maxIterations is the only thing that stops it.
